diagnosis: Composite expansion copies every leaf-model DEFAULT param into subProps via setModelParam, which marks it as user-given. For the phototransistor BJT spice model this marks RCO (default 0.01) as given, so bjt.ts:1549 rcoGiven becomes true and the BJT setup splits off the Kull quasi-sat colPrime internal node (bjt.ts:1756). ngspice deck has no RCO so it splits no node. Result is an extra MNA equation (12 vs 11), an orphan node tx:bjtPhoto:collector, a singular subsystem, non-convergence, and NaN node voltages so converged stays false.
fixHint: Do not mark leaf-default model params as given during composite expansion. Use a non-given default-seeding path so isModelParamGiven only reports params explicitly set in the netlist or by the user, matching standalone placement.

The extra equation and orphan node tx:bjtPhoto:collector make our DCOP diverge while ngspice converges, so the paired step-end node comparison withinTol is false for every node.

diagnosis: The test expects clamping gmMax to change the converged V_out, but gmMax is a Jacobian-only quantity. In load() the Norton offset iNR = iOutNow - gmEff*vDiff (ota.ts:226) plus the gmEff stamp contribution gmEff*vDiff cancel exactly at the NR fixed point, so the net injected output current equals iOut = iBias*tanh(vDiff/2vt), which is independent of gmMax. setParam updates this.p.gmMax correctly (ota.ts:254) and the clamp is applied (ota.ts:223), but the converged V_out = iOut*rLoad is unchanged, so before equals after (difference 0). The test asserts a false contract.
fixHint: This is a test-contract error not a source bug: gmMax only bounds the linearization slope and cancels at the fixed point. Either assert on convergence iteration behavior or remove the not.toBeCloseTo expectation; do not alter ota.ts load() which matches the VCCS companion model.

diagnosis: createAnalogFuseElement (and buildAnalogFuseElement constructor) never reads the blown property to seed _intact, which is hardcoded true at line 103. Building with blown:true still uses _rCold (0.01), so the fuse is conducting and rBlown is inert; hotloading rBlown via setParam changes a value that never enters the stamp, leaving output voltage unchanged (before==after).
fixHint: Seed _intact from props.blown: add a blown param to AnalogFuseElement constructor (initializing _intact = !blown) and pass props.getOrDefault blown false through buildAnalogFuseElement and createAnalogFuseElement so a fuse built blown uses _rBlown.

diagnosis: The DC-OP node voltage at rLoadC:pos is 0.9998 (ours) vs 0.9999 (ngspice); harness_first_divergence localizes to the dcopInitFloat contact-path conductance being 100 (ours, one contact closed) vs 200 (ngspice, both contacts closed in parallel). Cause: digiTS models the relay normally-closed contact by inverting CSW conduction at current-controlled-switch.ts:311 (closed = !energised when normallyClosed), so when energised the NC contact opens. ngspice CSW has no normallyClosed concept; the harness netlist generator emits the NC W-card as a plain CSW with only an ON keyword (netlist-generator.ts:905), which sets cold-start state but does NOT invert conduction, so the ngspice NC contact stays conducting in parallel with NO and doubles the path conductance.
fixHint: This is an architectural divergence to escalate, not a numerical bug: the normallyClosed inversion in current-controlled-switch.ts:311 has no ngspice CSW counterpart and the ON keyword at netlist-generator.ts:905 is not equivalent to it. To make the paired fixture comparable, the NC contact must be generated against an ngspice topology that actually inverts conduction (e.g. a complementary control source or a different device), or the relay-dt NC contact must be modeled without the digiTS-only inversion. Present both options to the user.

diagnosis: The fixture wires two non-tri-state digital drivers onto rf:Rw (the Splitter output spl:0,1 and the In in_rw:out), so the compiler (compiler.ts:486-531) registers Rw as a multi-driver bus net with both as hard drivers. resolveBusDrivers compares the two driver shadow-net values across all 32 bits with the XOR at bus-resolution.ts:75 and latches a burn on any differing bit; there is no masking of each driver contribution to the net declared bit width (addrBits=2) and no adapter-boundary precedence for a Splitter-output-into-narrow-port sharing a net with a direct In. Bits above the 2-bit address width (or a transient mismatch where the In shadow updates before the Splitter re-evaluates) trigger Bus conflict on net 1 even though the intended low-2-bit values agree.
fixHint: Mask each driver value and non-highZ mask to the net declared bit width before the conflict XOR at bus-resolution.ts:75 (carry the net width into resolveBusDrivers and AND driverValue/driverNonHighZ with the width mask), so phantom upper-bit disagreements between a narrow In and a Splitter output on the same net cannot latch a burn. Confirm by checking whether the In and Splitter shadow nets carry bits beyond addrBits at settle.

diagnosis: Test runs analysis dcop only (no transient step) then reads d1 state1 VD, expecting >0.5. state0->state1 copy lives only in _seedFromDcop (analog-engine.ts:2282), which fires on the dcop->transient transition during step(), never in a pure-dcop run. So after dcop alone state1 stays construction-zero and state1VD===0. ngspice likewise copies CKTstate1=CKTstate0 only at the dctran transient transition (dctran.c:349-350), not at end of dcop.
fixHint: Either the test must take one accepted transient step before reading state1 (its name says after accepted transient step but it builds with analysis dcop and never steps), or dcop convergence must seed state1 from state0. Decide contract; if test name is authoritative, add a step before the state1 read. If dcop should seed state1, add a state0->state1 copy at dcop convergence.

diagnosis: Rail-saturated unity follower (Vin=16) never converges (dcOperatingPoint returns converged=false, 3124 iters, all nodes 0). The load() mode switch at line 541 replaces the entire gain-stage Jacobian coupling with a constant RHS outputClampLevel*G_out whenever the iterate vOut>=vRailPos (line 509), while railLim (newton-raphson.ts:343-348) only bisects on a crossing and never pins to the rail. Once saturated the feedback coupling is removed from the Jacobian so the next iterate falls below the rail flipping outputSaturated off, re-adds coupling, overshoots above the rail again. The discontinuous saturated/unsaturated stamp switch has no fixed point so NR chatters.
fixHint: Saturation must be enforced as a smooth/limited continuation of the same Jacobian (keep the gain-stage coupling and add a soft clamp / push the rail limit through railLim only) instead of a hard branch that drops the -aEff*G_out coupling terms; the saturated branch at 541-542 produces a discontinuous Jacobian that prevents NR convergence.

diagnosis: Same root cause as dcop_rail_saturation: with Vin=16 the warm-start/DCOP for the rail-saturated unity follower does not settle into the saturated region, so OUT_SAT_FLAG (written at line 565 from outputSaturated) stays 0 instead of 1. outputSaturated is computed at lines 509-515 from the post-railLim iterate vOut, but the discontinuous saturated-branch stamp switch at 541 prevents the iterate from converging at/above vRailPos=13.5, so the flag is never latched.
fixHint: Fix the non-convergent saturation branch (line 541-542) so the saturated operating point is a stable NR fixed point at vOut=vRailPos; then OUT_SAT_FLAG written at 565 will latch to 1.

diagnosis: Same root cause: Vin=16 rail-saturated unity follower is stepped 5 times then OUT_SAT_FLAG is asserted to be 1 but reads 0. The discontinuous saturated/unsaturated stamp branch at line 541 (gain coupling dropped on saturation, railLim only bisecting on crossing) prevents the output from settling at/above vRailPos so outputSaturated (509-515) never becomes true and the flag written at 565 stays 0.
fixHint: Make the saturated operating point a stable fixed point (see line 541-542 fix); flag latching then follows.

diagnosis: Same root cause: Vin=16 overdrive DCOP does not converge into the rail region; output node settles around 1.6V (not >=13.499). The non-convergent saturated/unsaturated stamp switch at line 541 means railLim never produces a stable clip at vRailPos and OUT_SAT_FLAG stays 0, so the test that asserts the node reached the rail (>13.499) fails. railLim itself (newton-raphson.ts:343-348) is correct but its bisect-on-crossing behavior combined with the dropped gain coupling cannot reach a saturated fixed point.
fixHint: Same fix as line 541-542: keep the gain-stage Jacobian coupling under saturation so NR converges to vOut=vRailPos; railLim event recording at 488-502 then fires correctly.

diagnosis: Unity follower Vout reads 2.00098 not ~2.00000. buildUnityFollower (test line 45) passes no vos so the model default vos=1e-3 (param default at real-opamp.ts:170) applies. The closed-loop output is (Vin+vos)*aol/(1+aol) = 2.001 - 20uV ~ 2.00098, so the +0.00098 offset is exactly the +1mV default input offset voltage stamped via the gain stage RHS at line 555 (aEffScaled*G_out*p.vos). The test assumed vos=0 (its 0.0005 finite-gain-error budget) but the behavioral default is 1mV.
fixHint: Either the param default vos at real-opamp.ts:170 (1e-3) is non-zero while the test fixture assumes 0, or the gain-stage vos term at line 555 should not be applied at this magnitude; reconcile the default vos with the unity-follower closed-form the test asserts (vos belongs in the offset, so the 1mV default produces the observed 1mV shift).

diagnosis: Same default-vos issue: the before assertion expects Vout close to 2 within 0.0005 but reads 2.00098. buildUnityFollower passes no vos so default vos=1e-3 (real-opamp.ts:170) shifts the unity-follower output by +1mV via the gain-stage RHS at line 555. The 0.00098 delta is the 1mV offset minus the 20uV finite-gain error.
fixHint: Reconcile default vos (real-opamp.ts:170) with the test fixtures finite-gain-error budget; the +1mV default offset is the source of the failing before assertion.

diagnosis: Test sets vos=0.5 then steps 5 times (transient) and expects unity-follower Vout to shift by ~0.5V; actual shift is only 0.155V. In transient (geq_int>0) the gain-stage normal branch uses aEff=aol/(1+g) (line 459) which is a heavily attenuated effective gain, and the vos contribution to the output RHS at line 555 is aEffScaled*G_out*p.vos, so the offset is amplified by the reduced aEff rather than the full open-loop aol. After only 5 small steps the integrator (vInt) has not slewed enough to realize the full 0.5V offset, and the transient vos RHS term uses the bandwidth-reduced aEff. The closed-loop offset is therefore far below 0.5V.
fixHint: In the transient gain-stage the vos offset must propagate through the same loop gain as the steady-state DC path; the line-555 RHS uses aEff (bandwidth-reduced) for vos rather than the integrator-mediated full offset, so the 5-step transient under-realizes the offset. Either drive to DCOP or carry vos through vInt (line 454) consistently into the output without the aEff attenuation at 555.

diagnosis: Inverting amp aol=100 DCOP before reads -0.900 but test expects -0.0908. The source gain stage stamps a Norton VCVS with -aol*G_out / +aol*G_out coupling (lines 553-554) and diagonal G_out (533), which realizes the closed-loop gain -Vin*Rf/Rin * aol/(NG+aol) = -1*100/111 = -0.900 (NG=1+Rf/Rin=11). The test expects the textbook finite-gain formula -aol/(1+aol*NG) = -100/1101 = -0.0908. The two differ in where the noise gain NG multiplies aol: the source produces aol/(NG+aol), the standard op-amp finite-gain model produces aol/(1+aol*NG). The gain-stage Jacobian coupling (533/553/554) does not reproduce the standard finite-open-loop-gain closed-loop transfer.
fixHint: The gain-stage Norton formulation (diagonal G_out at 533 plus -aol*G_out coupling at 553) yields closed-loop aol/(NG+aol) instead of aol/(1+aol*NG). To match the standard finite-aol op-amp the gain element should be a high-gain VCVS into the output node (output forced ~ aol*vDiff) rather than aol scaling only the cross-coupling against a unit-G_out diagonal; reconcile the gain-stage stamp with the intended finite-gain transfer function.

diagnosis: Preset-vs-default DC output delta at Vin=4 reads 0.000536 but test expects 0.0005 within 5e-6. The preset OPA2134 changes both vos (0 -> 500e-6) AND aol (100000 -> 1e6). The +36uV excess over the pure vos delta is the finite-gain-error difference between the two aol values at Vin=4: the source closed-loop gain aol/(NG+aol) (gain stamps at 533/553) gives error ~Vin/aol per the Norton gain formulation, so error_default - error_preset = 4/100000 - 4/1e6 ~= 36uV. The delta is dominated by vos but the aol change contributes a real, non-negligible 36uV that the 5-decimal assertion does not budget for.
fixHint: The 36uV is genuine finite-gain-error physics from the gain stamp (533/553); either the test must budget the aol-induced gain-error difference (it only accounted for vos), or the gain-stage transfer differs from the assumed model. Same gain-stamp area as hotload_aol; reconcile the finite-gain transfer.

diagnosis: _computeZenerTp (zener.ts:230-248) recomputes the temperature-scaled thermal voltages vt nVt nbvVt and tBV but never derives a temperature-scaled saturation current (ngspice DIOtSatCur diotemp.c:175-185 = IS * exp((T/TNOM-1)*EG/vt) * (T/TNOM)^(XTI/N)). Match the diode element which already scales IS in dioTemp.

diagnosis: Same root cause as hotload_TEMP_changes_vf_forward but via the engine-driven computeTemperature path (setCircuitTemp(400) -> computeTemperature -> _computeZenerTp at zener.ts:525-529). _computeZenerTp (zener.ts:230-248) scales the thermal voltages but never derives a temperature-scaled saturation current tIS (DIOtSatCur diotemp.c:175-185). diagnosis: getPinCurrents (ntc-thermistor.ts:292-304) unconditionally reads tOld from the TEMPERATURE pool slot states[1][SLOT_TEMPERATURE] (line 298-300) instead of mirroring load()s non-self-heating branch which uses the live _tAmbient field (line 264). diagnosis: CORRECTION (supersedes prior medium record): direct probe confirms load() recomputes the latch and writes s0[CONDUCTING] every step but state1[CONDUCTING] never updates from its boot value. diagnosis: The AM evaluation at ac-voltage-source.ts:498 (dcOffset + (1+depth*sin(2pi*modFreq*t))*amplitude*sin(arg)) is mathematically correct and matches the documented contract. buildFixture takes exactly ONE warm-start step (build-fixture.ts:137) landing at t=firstStep ~3.3e-8 s (back-solved from the observed vSine=0.00104720 = 5*sin(2pi*1000*t)). At that tiny t the AM envelope deviation depth*sin(2pi*100*t) ~2e-5 so vAm-vSine ~1e-7 which is below the toBeCloseTo(...,4) threshold of 5e-5. The values DO differ (1.097e-7) confirming AM is applied; the assertion of 4-digit divergence is unsatisfiable at the warm-start sample time. The defect is the test sampling time/precision not an incorrect source line.
fixHint: This is a test-expectation issue: either step the fixture to a non-trivial t (e.g. a few modulation periods, t ~ 1/modFreq) before reading, or relax the AM divergence assertion to the achievable magnitude (~1e-7) / assert relative difference. The AM formula at line 498 is correct and should not be changed.

diagnosis: Composite sub-element PropertyBag is seeded from leaf model defaults via subProps.setModelParam(k,v), which marks every default-valued param as GIVEN (properties.ts:164 adds to _mparamsGiven). The BJT sub-elements therefore see rcoGiven=true (RCO defaults 0.01), so each BJT allocates a Kull quasi-saturation epi node (bjt.ts setup makeVolt collCX path) plus the IRCI epi branch (IRCI_VRCI=1/RCO=100). diagnosis: Triac never latches: harness shows ours V_MT2=4.99999999924 (full source, I=7.6e-12 A) vs ngspice latched 0.909 V. diagnosis: Cached VPK slot (247.22459) differs from the final converged node difference vP-vK (247.22386) by 0.73 mV vs the 5e-10 tolerance. load() writes s0[SLOT_VPK]=vpkNew where vpkNew=vP-vK reads ctx.rhsOld (line 263) - the PRE-solve linearization voltage that produced THIS iterations stamps. The matrix is then solved producing new node voltages. After the last converged iteration the cached slot therefore holds the second-to-last solutions bias point, one NR step (0.73mV) behind engine.getNodeVoltage(V1:P). This lag is intrinsic SPICE behavior (state slots cache the bias used for linearization, not the post-solve node difference), matching ngspice *vpk=vpk semantics.

diagnosis: Behavioral D-FF preset yields ~Q=1 when Q=1 (expected ~Q=0). The edge detector uses a continuous product risingEdge = vClock*(1-prevClock) plus an instance-field _firstSample (line 53) instead of the NaN sentinel in SLOT_LAST_CLOCK that the schema doc on line 28 specifies. _firstSample is reset on the first load() across NR/timesteps and not pool-backed, so the rising edge is mis/pre-consumed and q is not latched to vD; ctrl_nq=1-q then drives ~Q high. netlist mapping (d.ts:139-140 qPin<-ctrl_q, nqPin<-ctrl_nq) is correct, so the defect is in the driver edge arithmetic/first-sample handling.
fixHint: Replace the multiplicative edge detector and instance-field _firstSample with the documented NaN-sentinel scheme in SLOT_LAST_CLOCK: detect a true 0->1 edge against the prior accepted-step clock and skip detection only when LAST_CLOCK is NaN; latch q=vD only on a genuine rising edge so ctrl_nq=1-q is 0 when Q=1.

diagnosis: For the spice-l1 fixture GAMMA defaults to 0 and the body is tied to source (vbs=0, setup line 909). With GAMMA=0 the von threshold computation von=tVbi*polarity+GAMMA*sarg (mos1load.c:507) has no PHI dependence at nominal temperature (the body-effect term vanishes and tPhi approximately equals PHI when TEMP=TNOM), so changing PHI leaves the operating point bit-identical. The setParam/computeTemperature hot-load path itself works (KP/LAMBDA/VTO hot-load pass). This is ngspice-correct physics; the test asserts an effect that does not exist for a 3-terminal NMOS with GAMMA=0.
fixHint: Source is ngspice-correct (mos1load.c von formula). The test needs a fixture with nonzero GAMMA and a nonzero Vbs so PHI feeds the body-effect term; otherwise PHI has no OP effect. No source line change is correct.

diagnosis: Body tied to source (vbs=0, setup line 909) makes the GAMMA body-effect contribution cancel: von=tVbi*polarity+GAMMA*sarg where tVbi (mos1temp.c:170-176, computed line 427) contains -polarity*GAMMA*sqrt(PHI); at vbs=0 and nominal temp sarg=sqrt(tPhi) approximately sqrt(PHI), so the +GAMMA*sarg term cancels the -GAMMA*sqrt(PHI) inside tVbi. Net von is GAMMA-independent, leaving vd bit-identical. This is correct ngspice behaviour (body effect is zero at Vbs=0); the test asserts an effect that cannot occur for a 3-terminal device with grounded body.
fixHint: Source matches ngspice. The test must apply a nonzero source-body bias (Vbs!=0) to expose GAMMA; with body=source GAMMA legitimately has no OP effect. No source line change is correct.

To reach bit-exact match the step-0 DC-OP RHS accumulation order for the TRA MODEDC bridge plus the load resistor must be reordered to match ngspices traload.c MODEDC stamp emission order into the shared internal node row. Escalate as arithmetic-order divergence not a numerical model bug.

diagnosis: After an accepted DC-steady-state step the test requires state0[C1.Q]===state1[C1.Q] strictly (Object.is). The capacitor recomputes its charge each NR iteration as s0[Q]=C*vcap where vcap=v0-v1 is the freshly NR-solved terminal voltage (line 582). state1[Q] is the rotated copy of the prior step charge. The two NR solves of the identical steady-state system settle on node voltages that differ by 1 ULP so C*vcap yields a Q 1 ULP off (4.999999999999998e-6 vs 4.9999999999999996e-6); strict equality fails. The divergence is in the live-voltage charge recompute, not the rotation.
fixHint: The invariant cannot be met through the C*vcap path while node voltages carry 1-ULP solver roundoff between identical steps. Either the recompute must reuse the rotated charge when the converged voltage is unchanged (predictor/INITPRED-style copy s1->s0) so state0 is bit-identical, or the strict slot-equality invariant must be re-derived against what ngspice actually guarantees at steady state. Escalate the disposition.

diagnosis: Same root cause as pool_state0_state1_agree: across repeated accepted DC-steady-state steps the test asserts state0[C1.Q]===state1[C1.Q] strictly each step. The capacitor charge is recomputed from the live NR-solved terminal voltage (s0[Q]=C*vcap, vcap=v0-v1) rather than from the rotated prior charge, so each NR solve of the identical system lands 1 ULP off (step1: 4.999999999999996e-6 vs 4.999999999999998e-6). Strict equality fails on the capacitor Q slot.
fixHint: Identical to the companion test: make the steady-state charge bit-stable (reuse rotated charge when the converged voltage is unchanged, INITPRED-style s1->s0 copy) or re-derive the invariant against ngspices actual steady-state guarantee. Escalate disposition; do not weaken the assertion.

diagnosis: After hotloading vIH=4.0 the CLK thresholder outputs the 0.5V indeterminate level for a 3.3V clock (0.8<3.3<4.0). The instant-mode EOC-clear branch tests vClock < 0.5 which is false at exactly 0.5, so EOC never clears and stays true. The driver treats the indeterminate 0.5V clk_result as logic-high.
fixHint: clk_result carries logic levels 0.0/0.5/1.0 from DigitalInputThresholder. The driver must classify clk high only when vClock > 0.5 (above indeterminate midpoint) for edge detection at line 235-237 and clear EOC when vClock <= 0.5 at line 290, instead of the raw-product edge test and the strict < 0.5 compare.

diagnosis: CLK=1.5V is between vIL=0.8 and vIH=2.0 so the DigitalInputThresholder outputs the 0.5V indeterminate level on clk_result. The driver edge test risingEdge = vClock*(1-prevClock) = 0.5*(1-0) = 0.5 > 0 fires an edge, so instant-convert latches code 128 instead of leaving it at 0.
fixHint: The driver must treat clk_result as logic-high only when vClock > 0.5 (above the indeterminate midpoint produced by DigitalInputThresholder which emits 0.0/0.5/1.0). Replace the raw vClock*(1-prevClock) edge test at line 235 with a thresholded-level rising-edge detection so the 0.5V indeterminate band is not counted as high.

diagnosis: CCVSAnalogElement.setParam only handles senseSourceLabel and silently ignores transresistance. The transresistance is baked into the compiled expression (transresistance * I(sense)) inside the factory at lines 388-391, and the element holds the precompiled expr/deriv with no recompile path, so setComponentProperty(transresistance,2000) is a no-op and V_out stays at 1.0.

diagnosis: ComparatorDriver stamps a RAIL-LEVEL Thevenin target vTarget = (1-w)*vOH + w*vOL onto the internal ctrl_out net, but the downstream DigitalOutputPinLoaded.outPin expects ctrl in NORMALIZED [0,1] V (digital-output-pin-loaded.ts:8-11) and re-applies the rail mapping via its eDrive VCVS gain=(vOH-vOL). The rail span is applied twice, so ctrl_out near vOH=5V is multiplied again, driving cmp:out far over rail (15.87V instead of 3.3V).
fixHint: The driver must stamp the NORMALIZED logic level onto ctrl_out, not the rail-level vTarget. With latch=0 inactive the logic level is 1 (-> outPin drives vOH) and latch=1 active is 0 (-> vOL), so stamp (1 - wForStamp) as the ctrl target at line 193, letting the outPins eDrive VCVS do the single rail mapping.

diagnosis: weightBefore and weightAfter are both 1. The responseTime transient test then has no headroom: weight is already saturated at 1 so it cannot increase further, and the greater-than assertion fails.

test: parallel split at junction: branch wires carry their branch current
diagnosis: ComponentCurrentPath carries no element identity (eIdx or label) and resolve() builds _componentPaths skipping elements with fewer than 2 pins (Ground continues at line 302). getComponentPaths() therefore returns fewer paths than elementToCircuitElement has entries and in a non-aligned order, so the test positional pairing paths[pIdx] to the pIdx-th element map entry drifts past the skipped Ground and compares r1 body current against the wrong element current, giving relative error near 0.4.
fixHint: Add an element identity field (elementIndex or label) to ComponentCurrentPath and populate it at the push sites in resolve() (lines 298 and 304) so consumers map a body path to its element by identity instead of positional index.

test: every junction wires carry positive current and KCL is satisfied
diagnosis: Same root cause as the parallel-split body-path test: ComponentCurrentPath has no element identity and resolve() omits paths for 1-pin elements (Ground), so getComponentPaths() is shorter and misaligned versus elementToCircuitElement. The 8-element ladder pairing paths[pIdx] against the pIdx-th element drifts after the skipped Ground, comparing a body path current to the wrong element current and producing relative error near 0.95. The KCL wire-current asserts in the same test pass; only the positional body-path mapping fails.
fixHint: Attach an element identity (elementIndex or label) to ComponentCurrentPath at the resolve() push sites so the body-path-to-element mapping is by identity, not positional index that breaks when 1-pin elements are skipped.

test: RC step response: exponential charging matches V(1-e^-t/tau)
diagnosis: The warm-start transient DC operating point (_transientDcop -> solveDcOperatingPoint -> _seedFromDcop) is deferred to the first step() via the if (!this._firstStep) gate instead of running at init/compile time. The test compiles with Vs=0, hot-changes Vs to 5 via setSignal, then calls stepToTime; the deferred op runs after the hot change, so the capacitor is initialized to the Vs=5 steady state (cap open, node=5V) at t=0. vcAtTau therefore reads the DC steady state 4.999999999999995 instead of the 3.16V charging value.
fixHint: Run the initial transient operating-point solve at init()/compile() time (capturing the source value present then) so a later hot setSignal followed by step() continues the transient from the established reactive-element state rather than re-seeding _seedFromDcop at the changed source value.

test: RL step response: V_R matches 1-e^-t/tau (R=10 L=1mH tau=100us)
diagnosis: Same deferred-warm-start-DCOP root cause as the RC step test. The transient operating point seeded by _seedFromDcop runs only on the first step() (gated by if (!this._firstStep)), which happens after the test hot-changes Vs from 0 to 1 via setSignal. The op then initializes the inductor to its Vs=1 steady-state current (inductor shorted), so V_R reads the full 1V drop at t=tau instead of the 0.632V rising-current value.
fixHint: Run the initial transient operating-point solve at init()/compile() time so the inductor state is established before the hot setSignal; the subsequent step() must continue the transient from that state rather than re-running _seedFromDcop at the changed source value.

diagnosis: After buildFixture warm-start (one transient step) the last-accepted W lives in s1 because rotateStateVectors at the top of the next step recycles states[0]. The second dcOperatingPoint() then re-enters DC mode where load() reads wOld = s0[W] (line 226), which is the recycled/stale buffer not the valid last-accepted W, so DCOP linearizes around a wrong W (driving R toward rOn) and V(mem:neg)=0.834 instead of the w=0.5 value 0.1105.
fixHint: In DC mode the memristor must read the last-accepted W from s1 (like the inductor), not s0. Reading s0 only holds the seed on the FIRST load before any rotation; after a warm-start rotation the valid value is in s1. Resolve W from s1 (or seed s1 too) for the DC branch at line 226.

diagnosis: On LTE rejection the engine (analog-engine.ts:625-636) only rewinds simTime and sets dt=newDt; it deliberately does NOT restore the state pool (per the ngspice dctran.c:935-954 comment at line 626-629). So after a rejected step the NR-written s0 differs from the last-accepted s1 on the crystal leaf reactive slots (PHI/Q), giving -5.14e-8 vs -3.22e-8. The test asserts s0==s1 (rollback invariant) which the engine never establishes on the LTE-reject path.
fixHint: Either the rejection path must restore state pool s0 from s1 before the retry to honor the asserted invariant, or the test invariant is wrong (ngspice intentionally does not restore state on LTE reject). Escalate: this is a state-rotation-on-reject semantics decision, not a crystal-component bug.

diagnosis: The test asserts an LTE rejection MUST occur for the square-wave RL (L=1e-3,R=1,500Hz,5ms,free-running maxStep), then reads it. No step in the convergence log has lteRejected===true so the lookup returns undefined and toBeDefined fails. shouldReject (timestep.ts:616, reject when worstRatio>=1/0.9) is ngspice-faithful; cktTerr never produced a ratio over 1.111 on this circuit so no rejection fired. The fixture params simply do not drive the LTE controller past the reject threshold.
fixHint: Test-precondition gap, not a source bug: shouldReject and cktTerr match ngspice. To exercise the rollback path the fixture needs sharper flux gradients or smaller minTimeStep so worstRatio exceeds 1.111, or the test must not assume a rejection always occurs. Escalate if a rejection is required by spec.

diagnosis: Composite sub-element construction writes leafEntry model-registry DEFAULT params via setModelParam which MARKS THEM GIVEN. The DIAC diode sub-elements thus get ISR=1e-14 flagged as given so diode setup _recSatCurGiven becomes true. At INITJCT vd=tVcrit=66.379 the recombination arm at diode.ts:1031 computes exp(66.379/(NR*vt=0.0517))=exp(1284)=Infinity then cdeq=cd-gd*vd=Inf-Inf=NaN. NaN floods the matrix so the engine never converges (2300 iters vs ngspice 5). ngspice keeps DIOrecSatCurGiven false and gets finite cd=0.7315.
fixHint: Write leafEntry.params (model-registry defaults) with non-given semantics (replaceModelParams without markGiven or a setModelParam variant that does not add to _mparamsGiven); only subEl.params explicit overrides should be marked given. This stops ISR/NR/ISW/JTUN defaults from activating arms ngspice keeps off.

diagnosis: Same root cause as init_blocking_node_voltage_near_zero: composite sub-element diode defaults ISR/NR are wrongly marked given so the recombination arm (diode.ts:1031) is active and overflows at vd=tVcrit. diagnosis: Harness on transformer-canon-ac-step-down.dts shows our engine runs only 1 step then stalls (lastDt=0, did not advance) while ngspice runs 1009 steps. Our DCOP limps through 11 gminDynamic + 11 srcSweep attempts ALL accepted=false and we emit NO tranInit phase (ngspice emits 2 tranInit + tranPredictor/tranNR loop). The transient first-step dt seed (_timestep.currentDt = firstStep) and _seedFromDcop MODEINITTRAN seeding at line 1083-1091 are GATED on result.converged; for the coupled-inductor transformer the DCOP does not return a clean converged op (two inductors shorted at DC + mutual coupling form a singular branch block), so currentDt stays at the line-1061 seed value of 0 and getClampedDt returns ~0 at step() line 351, collapsing the first timestep. Matrix divergence confirms our inductor branch diagonal stays at DC req=0 where ngspice has the transient companion -50000.
fixHint: Make the transient first-step seeding (currentDt=firstStep and _seedFromDcop) robust to the transformer coupled-inductor DCOP path so MODEINITTRAN and a nonzero first dt are established; root the upstream DCOP convergence for the singular coupled-inductor branch block so result.converged is true after source-sweep as it is in ngspice.

diagnosis: The diode series-resistance internal prime node is allocated in setup() ONLY when params.RS != 0 (line 839-843: RS==0 sets _posPrimeNode = posNode = anode, no internal node). The fixture builds the diode with default RS=0 so no internal node exists. setParam(RS,100) at line 1379-1390 updates params.RS and recomputes tConductance via computeTemperature, but _posPrimeNode stays the anode because the topology (internal node + its allocElement handles) is fixed at setup time. The series-resistance conductance gspr is therefore stamped anode-to-anode with no observable effect, so V(anode) changes by only 2.19e-10 (numerical noise) instead of the expected ~0.4V IR drop.
fixHint: Series-resistance RS is a topology-affecting param (needs an internal prime node). Hot-loading RS from 0 to nonzero cannot be honored by setParam alone; either always allocate the internal prime node and gspr stamps regardless of RS (degenerate to a short when RS=0) so RS becomes a pure stamp value, or trigger a recompile/re-setup when RS crosses 0. Mirror ngspice where DIOconductance is recomputed but the prime node always exists when the model can have RS.

diagnosis: BV hot-load does not propagate into the diode breakdown behavior. MCP evidence: building the same reverse-bias circuit with BV=5 at compile time gives anode=4.958V (breakdown engages); building with default BV=Inf then circuit_patch set BV=5 keeps anode=0.000V (breakdown never engages) even though setComponentProperty routes to el.setParam (coordinator.ts:916-917) which sets params.BV=5 and recomputes tBV via computeTemperature. The breakdown arm in load() therefore stays inactive after hot-load. Separately the test reads d1:K (cathode) which is hard-tied to the 10V source and can never change, so even a working BV change would be invisible at that node - but the underlying source bug is the BV hot-load not engaging breakdown (observable at the anode).
fixHint: Make the diode setParam(BV,...) path re-establish the breakdown regime: ensure the finite-BV branch flags/seed used at setup (and the warm-start junction voltage) are refreshed so the next solve re-enters the breakdown arm, matching the compile-time BV=5 result. Verify configure({}) re-runs setup or that load() reads the live params.BV/tBV rather than a setup-captured copy. (Test should also observe the anode, not the source-pinned cathode.)

Composite expansion (triac/scr/diac) leaks registry-default givenness: compiler.ts:515-518 uses setModelParam for leafEntry.params defaults which marks them given (properties.ts:164), flipping rcoGiven/diode-given true and allocating internal prime nodes ngspice never allocates. ngspice deck (netlist-generator.ts:1130 emits only given params) omits rco= and collapses the node, producing the A1 equation-count divergence ours 11 vs ngspice 7.

SCR composite expands into NpnBJT/PnpBJT spice leaves; compiler.ts:515-518 marks the BJT registry-default RCO=0.01 as given via setModelParam (properties.ts:164), so bjt.ts:1549 rcoGiven=true and bjt.ts:1753-1758 allocates a collector-prime node ngspice collapses. ours matrixSize exceeds ngspice by the per-BJT internal-node count.

Triac composite NpnBJT/PnpBJT spice leaves get RCO=0.01 marked given by the compiler.ts:515-518 leaf-default loop (setModelParam marks given at properties.ts:164), flipping rcoGiven true so bjt.ts:1753-1758 allocates collector-prime nodes ngspice never allocates. harness_topology_diff on triac-canon-blocking.dts confirms ours matrixSize 11 vs ngspice 7 (4 extra equations, 100=1/RCO diagonal cells).

diagnosis: TransGate compiles in the digital domain so its boundary source pins (vs/vp1/vp2/load) each get a BridgeOutputDriverElement. In Hi-Z the bridge stamps 1/rHiZ=1e-7 on its boundary node diagonal (bridge-output-driver.ts:154; the rOut path at :162 is the driven analog). ngspice models the same .dts as ideal V-sources with no such parasitic, so harness_matrix_diff reports a first-iter coord-set divergence at step0 iter0: ours has 7 matrix entries vs ngspice 5, with extra cells (2,2)=1e-7,(3,3)=1e-7 and (1,1) carrying +1e-7. Confirmed via harness_topology_diff (4 bridge ours-only elements) on trans-gate-canon-pass-through.dts.
fixHint: The bridge boundary drivers inject a parasitic 1/rHiZ (and 1/rOut) conductance that the ngspice deck has no counterpart for. Either suppress the bridge diagonal conductance stamp when the boundary pin is an ideal source (so the matrix matches the ideal-V-source deck), or have the netlist generator emit a matching parasitic. Localize the divergent cells with the 1e-7 stamps at bridge-output-driver.ts:154/162.

diagnosis: Same mechanism as paired_pass_through. The auto-generated ngspice deck models the source pins as ideal V-sources with no such conductance, so the step0 iter0 matrix coord-set differs (ours-only 1e-7 diagonal cells), tripping the Matrix-entry structural divergence assertion. Bridges are ours-only elements per harness_topology_diff.
fixHint: Same fix as pass_through: do not stamp the bridge boundary parasitic conductance for ideal-source boundary pins (or generate a matching parasitic on the ngspice side). Wrong cells are the 1e-7 stamps at bridge-output-driver.ts:154/162.

diagnosis: Test reads getElementPinCurrents on the cap:cBody Capacitor leaf expecting the DC leakage current V/(esr+rLeak)=2e-7A, but the Capacitor leaf returns CCAP (companion current) which is 0 at the DC operating point per capload.c:80-81 (DC branch only writes Q). The DC leakage current physically flows through the cap:rLeak resistor leaf, not the capacitor body, so cBody correctly reports 0.
fixHint: Test targets wrong leaf: probe cap:rLeak (or cap:rEsr) for DC series current, not cap:cBody. Source is correct; if a total-component current is wanted, read it off the wrapper or a resistive leaf.

diagnosis: Same root cause as the dc-current test: iBefore is read via getElementPinCurrents on cap:cBody, which returns CCAP=0 at the DC operating point (capacitor.ts:641, capload.c:80-81). The toBeCloseTo(2e-7,8) against the cBody current fails because cBody carries no DC current. Secondary latent bug: rLeak resistance is frozen at netlist-build time (polarized-cap.ts:200/213) as a literal number, so leakageCurrent is NOT a string-bound param and never re-derives rLeak on hot-load.
fixHint: Read DC current off cap:rLeak not cap:cBody. Also make rLeak resistance a function-form re-derivable binding so leakageCurrent/voltageRating hot-load updates R_leak (polarized-cap.ts:200).

diagnosis: iBefore read via getElementPinCurrents on cap:cBody returns CCAP=0 at DC operating point (capacitor.ts:641, capload.c:80-81); the toBeCloseTo(2e-7,8) fails. The DC current is in cap:rLeak. Additional latent bug: voltageRating is consumed only at netlist-build time to compute the literal rLeak resistance (polarized-cap.ts:194-200), so hot-loading voltageRating never updates R_leak.
fixHint: Probe cap:rLeak for DC current. Make rLeak resistance re-derivable on voltageRating/leakageCurrent change (polarized-cap.ts:200).

diagnosis: iBefore and iAfter are both read via getElementPinCurrents on cap:cBody, which returns CCAP=0 at DC (capacitor.ts:641, capload.c:80-81). The ratio Math.abs(iAfter)/Math.abs(iBefore) becomes 0/0 = Infinity, failing toBeCloseTo(2,1). M multiplicity at DC affects the resistive leakage branch (cap:rLeak/cap:rEsr), not the open cap body, so the assertion must read a resistive leaf.
fixHint: Read DC current off cap:rLeak (the conduction path that M scales at DC), not the open-circuited cap:cBody body.

diagnosis: Capacitor.setParam(capacitance) updates only _nominalC; the stamped capacitance this.C is recomputed solely in computeTemperature() (capacitor.ts:340/432), which a bare coordinator.step() after setComponentProperty never invokes. So the cBody stamp keeps the OLD capacitance and the post-hotload first-step current is identical (diff exactly 0), failing not.toBeCloseTo. Capacitance hot-load is inert until a temperature recompute fires. (Test also wrongly assumes first-step current is C-sensitive when it is esr-limited at tiny dt.)
fixHint: In capacitor.ts setParam, after setting _nominalC recompute this.C = _nominalC * _SCALE (or have the engine re-run computeTemperature on any param change) so capacitance is truly hot-loadable.

diagnosis: Test reads getElementPinCurrents on cap:cBody (the capacitor body) expecting it to track V/esr. esr does hot-load correctly (Resistor.setParam calls updateConduct at resistor.ts:595), but the cBody companion current (CCAP) only equals V/esr at the very first transient step when the cap is a near-short. After the warm-start step plus one more step() the cap is already charging, so cBody CCAP is no longer V/esr and stays ~2 rather than dropping to ~1. The assertion observes the wrong leaf/quantity.
fixHint: Read the ESR-limited current off cap:rEsr (the resistor in the series path), not the cap:cBody companion current, and read it at the first transient step before charging dominates.

diagnosis: harness_first_divergence earliest = matrix cell (nCap,nCap) at step0/iter0, classification value-only, single cell, absDelta=1.78e-15 (1 ULP). The nCap diagonal accumulates three += stamps: rEsr.G + rLeak.G + cBody.geq. The leaf load order our engine walks differs in associativity from ngspice (R,R load then C load) for this expanded composite, producing a 1-ULP sum on the shared diagonal. Per CLAUDE.md this is the per-device load() accumulation order on a shared diagonal upstream cause, not the sparse solver. The 1.1e-16 node-voltage delta is the downstream symptom.
fixHint: Align the per-leaf load() walk order for the PolarizedCap composite so rEsr/rLeak/cBody stamp the nCap diagonal in the same sequence ngspice loads them (RESload before CAPload); verify expandCompositeInstance/ngspiceLoadOrder leaf ordering matches the emitted SPICE deck.

diagnosis: events.length>=1 passes (line 573); the failing assertion is line 574 .some(e.junction===VD || e.junction.startsWith(V)). The diode _recordLimit hardcodes junction:AK (diode.ts:1258), an anode-cathode label that neither equals VD nor starts with V, so .some() returns false. The diode junction state slot is named VD (DIOvoltage, diodefs.h:196, declared at diode.ts:68) but the limiting record uses AK, an inconsistent junction identifier.

diagnosis: Test enables the convergence log via coordinator.setConvergenceLogEnabled(true) (coordinator.ts:482-491) AFTER buildFixture warm-started with logging off, then runs 200 coordinator.step() calls; getConvergenceLog() returns a non-null but EMPTY array (length 0), failing log.length>=1 at line 623. diagnosis: harness_run on the tapped-transformer ac-sinusoid fixture: our engine produces only 1 step then errors Our engine did not advance at step 0 (simTime=0 lastDt=0) with 49 NR iterations vs ngspice 1009 steps avg 2.26 iters. harness_matrix_diff classification value-only: every coupled-inductor branch self-term and mutual cross-term is 0 on our side while ngspice has the L*ag0 companion (-200000 self, -160000/-80000 mutual) at step0/iter0. Cause: the first transient dt is 0 so ag[0]=0, making inductor req=ni.geq=ag0*L=0 (inductor.ts:871) and mutual stamps -mutFactor*ag0=0 (mutual-inductor.ts:236-238); the branch-equation diagonal -req=0 leaves the coupled-inductor matrix degenerate and NR stagnates. Confirmed NOT tapped-specific: the plain transformer-canon-ac-step-down fixture stagnates identically. _transientDcop seeds currentDt=_params.firstStep (analog-engine.ts:1091); firstStep is resolving/seeding to 0 for inductor-branch transformer topologies.
fixHint: Trace why _params.firstStep (resolveSimulationParams) is 0 for these transformer transient runs so the first-step dt feeds a nonzero ag0; the inductor/mutual stamp code is ngspice-faithful and is not the bug. Engine first-transient-step dt seeding for branch-equation reactive elements is the fix site.

test: stamps vOH at out when in+ is above in- (not sinking: pull-up holds line high)
diagnosis: Open-collector comparator latch polarity is inverted: latch=1 (active/sinking, drives vOL/LOW) is set when vPlus >= vTh i.e. when in+ is ABOVE in-, but a comparator drives output HIGH when V+ > V-. So in+ above in- wrongly sinks the output low instead of letting the pull-up hold it high.
fixHint: Invert the latch transition conditions at lines 165-166: latch should go to 1 (sinking) when vPlus < vTl and release to 0 when vPlus >= vTh, so V+>V- yields not-sinking (HIGH) and V+<V- yields sinking (LOW).

test: stamps vOL at out when in+ is below in- (asserted: sinking pulls line low)
diagnosis: Same inverted open-collector latch polarity: with in+ below in- the code keeps latch=0 (not sinking) so it drives ctrl_out toward vOH instead of sinking. The 24.9V readback is the outPin DigitalOutputPinLoaded fighting the wrongly-driven ctrl_out net, a downstream symptom of the inverted latch.
fixHint: Invert latch transition at lines 165-166 so latch becomes 1 (sinking, drives vOL) when vPlus < vTl; that makes in+ below in- pull the open-collector output low through the external pull-up.

test: stamps vOH at out when in+ is above in- (push-pull drives HIGH)
diagnosis: Push-pull comparator latch polarity is inverted (mirrors comparator-driver.ts): latch=1 (drives vOL/LOW) is set when vPlus >= vTh i.e. in+ above in-, so the push-pull stage drives the output LOW (got 0) when a comparator with V+>V- should drive it HIGH.
fixHint: Invert the latch transition conditions at lines 146-147 so latch=1 (drive vOL) engages when vPlus < vTl and releases when vPlus >= vTh; V+>V- then yields w->0 and vTarget->vOH (HIGH).

test: stamps vOL at out when in+ is below in- (push-pull drives LOW)
diagnosis: Same inverted push-pull latch polarity: in+ below in- keeps latch=0 so the stage drives ctrl_out toward vOH; the outPin DigitalOutputPinLoaded then drives out to 24.9V (out of rail) fighting the wrongly-driven ctrl_out, a symptom of the inverted latch.
fixHint: Invert latch transition at lines 146-147 so latch=1 (vOL) engages when vPlus < vTl; in+ below in- then drives the push-pull output LOW as expected.

diagnosis: setComponentProperty routes a parent hot-load to only ONE flattened analog sub-element. _resolveElementIndex (coordinator.ts:712-719) builds a Map keyed by CircuitElement, so for a composite all sub-elements collapse to the same parent key and only the last-written index survives. The DAC vIH must reach 8 DigitalInputThresholder leaves (two composite levels deep via DigitalInputPinLoaded) but the single setParam call at line 915-918 never fans out, so vIH stays at default 2.0 and 3.3V still reads HIGH (V_OUT stays ~2.5 instead of 0).
fixHint: Make setComponentProperty fan out the param to ALL analog sub-elements whose CircuitElement is the target (iterate elementToCircuitElement collecting every idx for the element, call setParam on each), so composite-nested consumers like the per-bit thresholders receive vIH.

diagnosis: DACDriver stamps an ideal VSRC branch (V_OUT - V_GND = target exactly, lines 196-200) with no series output resistance, and buildDacNetlist (dac.ts) never wires the rOut param into any element. So the output is a stiff voltage source that cannot sag under load: V_OUT stays at 5.0 instead of 5*1e6/(1e6+1e3)=4.9950. The vIH/rOut hot-load also cannot take effect because DACDriver.setParam is a no-op (dac-driver.ts:159) and rOut is not consumed anywhere.
fixHint: Stamp a Thevenin output (target voltage behind rOut) in DACDriver.load instead of an ideal VSRC branch, and wire the rOut param into the driver so output impedance creates the divider; make rOut hot-loadable via setParam.

diagnosis: OpampElement.setParam writes only this._p[key], but load() computes the RES conductance G = 1/this._rOut using the separate constructor-cached field this._rOut (opamp.ts:194,258) which setParam never updates. Hot-loading rOut from 100 to 4000 mutates _p.rOut but the stamp keeps using stale _rOut=100, so V(out) stays at 9.0909 instead of changing to 2.0.
fixHint: In setParam, when key===rOut update this._rOut (or make load/G read this._p.rOut directly so the live value drives the conductance). The cached _rOut field and _p must not diverge.

diagnosis: Composite expansion copies the leaf model-entry DEFAULT params (subProps.setModelParam) which marks every default key as given; the optocoupler phototransistor NpnBJT thus reports isModelParamGiven(RCO)=true (bjt.ts:1549) and activates the Kull quasi-saturation branch (IRCI_VRCI=100, 1/RCI with RCO=0.01) that ngspice leaves off (IRCI_VRCI=0), stiffening the matrix so DCOP runs away (VBE=18V) and the collector node voltage becomes non-finite.
fixHint: Write leaf-entry default params with not-given semantics (replaceModelParams markGiven:false) so registry defaults are not flagged given; only the explicit subEl.params overrides should be marked given. Same bug at compiler.ts:295 in the node-allocation pass.

diagnosis: dc.converged is false: same root cause. Composite expansion marks the BJT leaf default RCO=0.01 as given (setModelParam at compiler.ts:517 always adds to _mparamsGiven), so rcoGiven=true at bjt.ts:1549 and the Kull QS branch (Irci_Vrci=1/tintCollResist=100, bjt.ts:2239) is stamped though ngspice keeps it 0; the stiff phantom branch prevents DCOP convergence (3448 NR iters, 76 attempts vs ngspice 12 iters).
fixHint: Copy leaf model-entry defaults with markGiven:false (replaceModelParams) so defaulted RCO is not given; keep explicit subEl.params overrides marked given. Mirror fix at compiler.ts:295.

diagnosis: cv.withinTol is false at step-end node compare: harness DCOP shows our engine fails to converge while ngspice converges, first divergence at step0 iter0 worstLabel tx:bjtPhoto:collector absDelta 17.5V. Root cause is the phantom Kull QS branch: composite expansion marks leaf default RCO as given (compiler.ts:517 setModelParam) so rcoGiven=true (bjt.ts:1549) and IRCI_VRCI=100 is stamped vs ngspice 0.
fixHint: Set leaf-entry default params not-given (replaceModelParams markGiven:false); only subEl.params overrides given. Fix compiler.ts:517 and the twin at compiler.ts:295.

diagnosis: Same root cause on the low-drive .dts fixture: cv.withinTol false because our DCOP diverges from ngspice. The optocoupler NpnBJT leaf gets its default RCO=0.01 marked given by composite expansion (compiler.ts:517 setModelParam always flags _mparamsGiven), so rcoGiven=true (bjt.ts:1549) activates the Kull quasi-saturation stamp (1/tintCollResist=100) absent in ngspice.
fixHint: Use replaceModelParams markGiven:false for leaf-entry defaults so defaulted RCO is not given; keep only explicit subEl.params overrides given. Apply to compiler.ts:517 and compiler.ts:295.

diagnosis: Number.isFinite(after) is false after stepping with raised CTR: the underlying DCOP/step never converges because the phototransistor BJT runs the phantom Kull QS branch. Composite expansion marks leaf default RCO=0.01 as given (compiler.ts:517 setModelParam adds to _mparamsGiven), so rcoGiven=true (bjt.ts:1549) and IRCI_VRCI=100 stamps a stiff branch ngspice never adds, producing non-finite node voltage.
fixHint: Copy leaf model-entry defaults with markGiven:false (replaceModelParams) leaving RCO not-given; mark only explicit subEl.params overrides given. Fix compiler.ts:517 and compiler.ts:295.

diagnosis: dc.converged is false: same root cause. The optocoupler phototransistor NpnBJT leaf default RCO=0.01 is wrongly flagged given by composite expansion (compiler.ts:517 setModelParam marks every default key given), so rcoGiven=true (bjt.ts:1549) and the Kull quasi-saturation branch (Irci_Vrci=1/tintCollResist=100, bjt.ts:2239) stamps a stiff phantom conductance ngspice keeps at 0, blocking DCOP convergence.
fixHint: Write leaf-entry default params not-given (replaceModelParams markGiven:false); only explicit subEl.params overrides marked given. Fix compiler.ts:517 plus the twin in the node-allocation pass at compiler.ts:295.

diagnosis: latchDrv drives ctrl_out (net 12) to a RAIL level q?vOH:vOL but that net feeds outPin DigitalOutputPinLoaded whose ctrl contract requires a NORMALIZED [0,1] logic level. outPin then computes nDriveV = vOL + ctrl*(vOH-vOL) = vOH*vOH so OUT settles super-rail (10.888 = approx 3.3*3.3) instead of 3.3V. Hot-load propagates fine; the absolute level is wrong due to double multiplication.
fixHint: latchDrv should stamp ctrl_out to a normalized logic level q?1:0 (line 129 ctrlTarget = q?1:0 with unit drive) so outPin maps it to the rail; remove the rail-level vOH/vOL drive from the latch driver and let outPin own the rail translation.

diagnosis: Same defect as vOH: latch driver feeds rail-level ctrl_out into outPin whose ctrl input must be normalized [0,1]. In the low state q=0 the latch stamps ctrl_out toward vOL but the comparator-fight / outPin re-mapping leaves OUT at 2.75V instead of the expected 0.5V; the low-rail translation belongs to outPin (DcVoltageSource vLowRail = vOL) not to a rail-level ctrl drive.
fixHint: Normalize ctrl_out to q?1:0 so outPin produces vOL when ctrl=0; let outPin own vOL via its vLowRail source rather than the latch driver double-driving the rail.

diagnosis: Same root cause: after lowering vOH to 4.0 OUT reads 12.124V (super-rail) so abs(after-4) > abs(after-5) fails. The latch driver drives ctrl_out to a rail voltage (q?vOH:vOL) that outPin then re-multiplies by gain (vOH-vOL), producing roughly vOH-squared instead of vOH at OUT.
fixHint: latchDrv must drive ctrl_out as a normalized [0,1] logic level (q?1:0); outPin then maps 1 to vOH. Then OUT high tracks vOH directly.

diagnosis: Verified via circuit_dc_op: t:DIS stays pinned at ~5V (VCC) regardless of disBase. disBase correctly tracks vDrop at build time (fresh build vDrop=3 gives node disBase=3.0V, vDrop=1.5 gives 1.5V) yet t:DIS is 5.0V in both cases because the discharge BJT (bjtDis, NpnBJT spice model) sinks ~0 collector current even at Vbe=3V, so DIS is decoupled from the base and the test sees only 1.5e-9 NR-reconverge noise. Separately the hot-load patch path left disBase frozen at 1.5 (setParam routing not reaching latchDrv), but the dominant failure is the non-conducting discharge transistor.
fixHint: The discharge BJT must saturate and pull DIS toward GND when the base is driven to vDrop. Investigate the bjtDis NpnBJT spice-model area/IS defaults and the disBase clamp interaction (latch-driver.ts:123-126 G_base=1 Norton) so collector current actually flows; the base-clamp-only scheme produces a base voltage but no collector conduction.

diagnosis: VCCSAnalogElement.setParam is an empty no-op (params underscored, no body), so setComponentProperty(transconductance, 0.02) is silently dropped and V(out+) stays at -1V instead of -2V. The element drives current from a parsed expression / cached gm built at construction; setParam never updates it.
fixHint: Implement setParam so key transconductance (and gain/m) rebuilds the linear expression / effectiveGm = transconductance*m used in stampOutput, mirroring how other controlled sources hot-reload; update the cached expression+derivative used by stampOutput.

test: Loaded: setComponentProperty(dipl, cIn, 1e-7) slows RC settling — binding is observable in transient
diagnosis: setParam(cIn) updates only this._spec.cIn but never the child AnalogCapacitorElement which holds its own capacitance baked at construction (line 62) and is what load() actually stamps (line 100-102). So hot-loading cIn=1e-7 does nothing; the RC keeps its default 1e-12 tau=1us and V(In_1) rises to ~2V at 5us instead of staying below 0.01V.
fixHint: In setParam case cIn forward the value to the child cap: this._capChild?.setParam(capacitance, value) (and reconcile when _capChild is null but value becomes >0). The spec field alone is not what gets stamped.

diagnosis: prevClock state slot initializes to 0. When C is set high before any step, the first sampleMonoflop sees clock=1 and prevClock=0 and the rising-edge test (clock!==0 && prevClock===0) fires a spurious trigger, so Q=1 instead of 0. There is no real 0->1 transition; the static high clock is misread as an edge because prevClock was never seeded with the initial clock level.
fixHint: Seed prevClock from the actual initial clock level so a pre-existing high does not register as a rising edge (e.g. initialize prevClock=clock on the first sample, or have the engine prime prevClock from initial inputs before the first executeFn). Edge must require a genuine low-to-high transition.

diagnosis: executeGraphicCard packs the D output (addr<<0 | str<<16 | clk<<17 | ld<<18 | bank<<19 = 983045 = 0xF0005) into the output slot with no mask to the dataBits bus width. dataBits=8 must truncate to the low byte (0xFF -> 5) but the function never reads dataBits and emits the full packed word, so D reads 983045 instead of 5.
fixHint: Mask the packed output to the data bus width: read dataBits via layout.getProperty(index, dataBits) and AND the result with ((1<<dataBits)-1) (handle dataBits>=32 as full width) before writing state[wt[outputIdx]].

diagnosis: AnalogClockElementImpl.setParam is an empty no-op so setComponentProperty(vdd, 5) is dropped and V(out) stays at 3.3V. Compounding it, _vdd and _halfPeriod are declared readonly (clock.ts:260-261) so they cannot be mutated even if setParam tried.
fixHint: Make _vdd (and _halfPeriod) mutable and implement setParam: key vdd updates _vdd, key Frequency recomputes _halfPeriod = 1/(2*value). The instantaneousValue/breakpoint math already reads these fields so the change becomes observable immediately.

diagnosis: AnalogClockElementImpl.setParam is an empty no-op so setComponentProperty(Frequency, 4000) is dropped; _halfPeriod stays at 0.5ms (1kHz). At t=0.2ms the element still reports the high half (3.3V) instead of the 4kHz low half (0V). _halfPeriod is also declared readonly (clock.ts:260) preventing mutation.
fixHint: Make _halfPeriod mutable and implement setParam so key Frequency recomputes _halfPeriod = 1/(2*value) (and vdd updates _vdd). nextBreakpoint/instantaneousValue read _halfPeriod so the new schedule takes effect immediately.
